human_approval_required_for: directory selection when no existing convention and no CLAUDE.md preference
force-removing a worktree with uncommitted changes (cleanup)
force-deleting a branch with unmerged commits (cleanup)
devloop_conventions: branch naming follows devloop workflow conventions (feature/, bugfix/, refactor/, migration/)
JS package-manager detection prefers pnpm-lock.yaml first (per project conventions), warns on mixed lock files
Python detection uses uv (per project conventions), not pip directly
cleanup reference for post-workflow worktree teardown

compositions.pairs_with: git-flow PR creation at workflow done; cleanup reference handles post-merge teardown

boundaries.produces: isolated worktree at a known path with a clean baseline
boundaries.does_not_produce: the implementation work that follows (caller's job)
boundaries.does_not_handle: merge conflicts on the parent branch (workflow's job at PR time), long-term stale-worktree management (see cleanup reference)
